en: She left her keys on the table and her coat is still here. Which sentence expresses the best deduction about the past?
He ____ ____ ____ the email — he replied within minutes.
The lights are off and nobody answered the door. Which fits the evidence?
You hear your neighbour's car start. Which expresses a logical present deduction?
A colleague looks exhausted every morning. Which is a cautious epistemic claim?
The fingerprints don't match. The suspect ____ ____ ____ the weapon.
Which sentence criticises a past failure (not deduction)?
There is no milk in the fridge and the carton is in the bin. Which deduction is natural?
He ____ ____ ____ abroad — he doesn't even own a passport.
Which expresses a past possibility that remains open (we are not sure)?
The sky is completely clear and the pavement is dry. Which is a sound deduction?
Given the tyre tracks in the snow, a vehicle ____ ____ ____ here minutes ago.
Which uses *must* for logical necessity (not obligation)?
A friend missed your party without warning. A sympathetic deduction might be:
The document is dated two years before his birth. He ____ ____ ____ it.
Which sentence shows *couldn't have* as confident negative deduction?
Choose the sentence that hedges appropriately in an academic discussion:
You're 99% sure she saw the message. She ____ ____ ____ it.
She was respected for her _______ — she refused to perform a persona that felt inauthentic.
Therapy helped her _______ a more coherent narrative of who she is.
He was deeply _______ — he could attune to others' emotions with unusual precision.
Many adolescents experience _______ about career choices — wanting two incompatible paths at once.
High _______ _______ is associated with lower anxiety when social feedback is ambiguous.
Online, people often _______ a polished version of the self.
Goffman described social life as _______ — we adjust self-presentation to the audience.
_______, she agreed to help, though she still had private doubts.
Years of setbacks tested her _______ — she recovered without losing her sense of purpose.
He struggled with _______ _______ despite objective success — fear of being exposed as a fraud.
The essay explores how _______ _______ shapes the stories we tell about our lives.
Constant _______ _______ on social media can erode self-esteem when context is stripped away.
She valued _______ _______ over popularity — acting on principles even when costly.
The interview examines how minority stress affects _______ _______ in young adults.
Critics argue that algorithmic feeds encourage _______ _______ rather than authentic exploration.
_______, he remained polite, but his tone suggested deep disagreement.
The course unit links _______ _______ with modal verbs of deduction.
_______ involves comparing your present self to an earlier version of you.

[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Our self-concept is actively constructed through social interaction and cultural narratives, not merely 'discovered' in isolation.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Goffman likened everyday life to theatre: we perform roles calibrated to each audience.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Narrative identity theory holds that people integrate life events into a revisable life story.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Self-concept clarity — a stable, well-defined sense of who one is — correlates with resilience.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Curated selfies and algorithmic feedback may amplify social comparison and self-doubt.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Authenticity is often framed as alignment between values and behaviour, not rigid consistency in every role.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Upward comparison can motivate improvement but undermine self-worth when contextual factors are ignored.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Impostor feelings are common among high achievers who attribute success to luck rather than ability.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Belonging cues from peers can buffer stress during identity exploration in emerging adulthood.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Minimal self-views emphasise continuity of consciousness rather than narrative richness.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Cultural norms prescribe which identity claims are legitimate or taboo in public discourse.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Intersectionality highlights that gender, class and ethnicity jointly shape self-understanding.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Defensive self-enhancement protects esteem but can block corrective feedback.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Identity plurality acknowledges multiple coherent selves across communities without pathology.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Temporal comparison can foster growth when prior setbacks are framed as learning.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Stereotype threat can undermine performance on tasks framed as diagnostic of ability.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Self-compassion treats setbacks with kindness rather than harsh self-criticism.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]
[[Text:|Texto:]] \"Identity work continues across the lifespan as roles and commitments evolve.\"\n\n[[According to the extract:|Según el extracto:]]

[[Speaker:|Voz / Hablante:]] \"People with clearer self-concepts often rebound faster after major life stressors.\"\n\n[[The speaker links clarity to:|La voz relaciona la claridad del autoconcepto con:]]
[[Speaker:|Voz / Hablante:]] \"A turning point narrative marks when someone felt they 'became who they really were'.\"\n\n[[The focus is:|El foco de la intervención es:]]
[[Lecturer:|Profesor/a (clase magistral):]] \"Upward comparison can erode self-esteem if we discount situational advantages.\"\n\n[[Main risk:|Principal riesgo:]]
[[Interviewer:|Entrevistador/a:]] \"Performative authenticity online can feel compulsory rather than liberating.\"\n\n[[The tone suggests:|El tono sugiere:]]
[[Coach:|Coach:]] \"Name the emotion beneath the self-criticism — that step reduces rumination.\"\n\n[[The advice targets:|El consejo apunta a:]]
[[Podcast host:|Presentador/a del podcast:]] \"Belonging isn't the same as fitting in — you can conform and still feel alone.\"\n\n[[Distinction:|Distinción que se pide:]]
[[Therapist:|Terapeuta:]] \"Values clarification exercises reduce decision paralysis tied to identity conflicts.\"\n\n[[Intended outcome:|Resultado pretendido:]]
[[Debate:|Debate:]] \"If identity is narrative, revision is a feature, not a failure.\"\n\n[[Speaker implies:|El hablante insinúa que:]]
[[Seminar leader:|Moderador/a del seminario:]] \"Hedging claims about others' motives avoids unfair moralisation.\"\n\n[[Pedagogical aim:|Objetivo (registro académico):]]
[[Radio:|Radio:]] \"Impostor feelings spike after promotions because the reference group shifts.\"\n\n[[Explanation:|Qué explica el fragmento:]]
[[Workshop:|Taller:]] \"Two-factor self-esteem models separate competence from worth.\"\n\n[[Conceptual point:|Punto conceptual:]]
[[Lecture:|Conferencia:]] \"Cultural tightness intensifies sanctioning of deviant self-presentations.\"\n\n[[Implication:|Implicación:]]
[[Clip:|Extracto de audio:]] \"Self-disclosure reciprocity builds trust faster than charisma alone.\"\n\n[[Mechanism:|Mecanismo:]]
[[Interview:|Entrevista:]] \"Identity fusion can explain extreme pro-group action.\"\n\n[[Key idea:|Idea clave:]]
[[Panel:|Mesa redonda:]] \"Microaggressions accumulate, creating hostile clarity about belonging.\"\n\n[[Effect described:|Efecto descrito:]]
[[Talk:|Charla:]] \"Future possible selves motivate study habits when vivid and plausible.\"\n\n[[Condition:|Condición:]]
[[Discussion:|Debate grupal:]] \"Labels can scaffold growth but also freeze self-stereotyping.\"\n\n[[Nuanced view:|Visión matizada:]]
[[Summary:|Resumen:]] \"Integration across minority identities can demand extra cognitive load daily.\"\n\n[[Point:|Idea que resume el fragmento:]]
Title: \"Cultural background is the single most important factor in shaping identity.\" Choose the strongest C1 opening.
Which paragraph uses contrast connectors most effectively?
Choose the most precise academic conclusion on identity formation.
Select the best thesis for a problem-solution essay on social comparison.
Which sentence avoids overstated certainty?
Pick the cohesive paragraph.
Choose appropriate register for a formal proposal.
Which reformulation avoids plagiarism while keeping meaning?
Best topic sentence for a body paragraph on digital self-presentation?
Select the sentence with parallel structure.
Which transition fits between paragraphs on theory and critique?
Choose the best definition-style sentence for *impostor phenomenon*.
Pick the most precise colligation in academic prose.
Which closing moves beyond summary to implication?
Choose the best hedged critique of a method.
Select the sentence that maintains an academic stance toward contested concepts.
Which option shows appropriate citation framing?
Choose the best sentence variety for rhetorical effect.
Concede a point before countering, in a formal debate:
Soften a strong claim in a seminar:
Structure a Part 3-style oral answer:
Politely disagree with a generalisation:
Buy time to think aloud:
Emphasise a key distinction orally:
Summarise someone else's view before responding:
Close a discussion with a balanced takeaway:
Ask for clarification in an academic tutorial:
Express partial agreement:
Redirect a hostile question:
Use a discourse marker to add weight:
Acknowledge limitations of your own argument orally:
Invite another speaker to contribute:
Respond to a request for examples:
Signal you are building on prior point:
Express empathy without overclaiming:
End with a forward-looking question (discussion):
